questions.question: Why does softmax attention have a noise floor that grows with context length?
options: Floating-point rounding errors

Softmax never produces exact zeros — every unrelated token gets O(1/N) mass, so at 128k context even 0.001% per token sums to ~12% of total weight; the model must route around a noise floor that grows with N
Longer prompts have more typos
The KV cache overflows past 32k
questions.explanation: Empirically it shows as hallucinated citations in long-context RAG, lost-in-the-middle failures, and degradation past 32k — the model wants a hard top-k that smooth softmax can't deliver.

questions.question: How does differential attention cancel the noise?
options: It clips small attention weights to zero

Split Q and K into two halves, compute two softmax maps, and output (A₁ − λ·A₂)V — the roughly uniform noise both maps share cancels in the subtraction, while the trained signal peaks appear in only one map and survive
It averages ten attention maps
It doubles the temperature
questions.explanation: Like a differential amplifier: common-mode noise subtracts out, differential signal remains. ICLR 2025 measured lower perplexity, better long-context accuracy, fewer hallucinations at equal size.

questions.question: What three problems kept DIFF V1 out of frontier pre-training?
options: Licensing, cost, and politics
The value cache loaded twice per decode step, custom CUDA kernels broke FlashAttention compatibility, and per-head RMSNorm destabilized long training runs at 70B+ scale
It only worked on encoder models
It required double the parameters
questions.explanation: V2 (Microsoft, January 2026) fixed all three: baseline-matching decode latency, no custom kernels, FlashAttention-compatible — the production-stack rewrite.

questions.question: Why doesn't the subtraction cancel the SIGNAL along with the noise?
options: Signal tokens are excluded from A₂
Cancellation requires the component to appear in BOTH maps at the same magnitude — at initialization the uniform noise does, but training drives the peaked signal weights into the maps asymmetrically
λ is set to zero for signal positions
The signal is added back afterwards
questions.explanation: The learned λ balances the two maps so the shared floor subtracts out while the differential part — what the model actually learned to attend to — passes through.

questions.question: What failure modes is differential attention specifically aimed at?
options: Slow tokenization of long documents

Attention-head interference at long context: hallucinated citations in RAG, lost-in-the-middle on 100k retrieval, and needle-in-haystack degradation past 32k — all downstream of unrelated-token noise accumulating
Out-of-memory errors during training
Repetition loops in short outputs
questions.explanation: As contexts stretched to 100k+, the O(1) total noise contribution became a first-order quality problem — which is why a noise-cancelling attention variant earned a production rewrite.
